observations.recommendation:
1.15 We recommended that:
❖ Management instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical
count of PPE in accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs,
Vol. 1. A representative of the Auditor be invited to witness the inventory
taking for this purpose;
❖ Inventory Committee -
• Prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE) which shall be reconciled with the PPE records
kept by the General Services Office and Accounting Office. A copy
thereof shall be submitted to COA for verification not later than January
31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS;
• Ensure that individual property number be provided and that Property
Sticker be duly accomplished and pasted on the prominent but secure
portion of the property for easy identification; and
❖ The General Services Officer maintain PPE Cards and ARE/PAR, and ensure
that the ARE/PAR be renewed every after three years pursuant to Section
492 of GAAM, Volume 1, to establish effective management and control over
PPE;
❖ The Municipal Accountant maintain complete and updated PPELC for each
category of PPE pursuant to Section 114 of NGAS Manual for LGUs to
provide ready information on the accounts and to support balances in the
financial statements; and
❖ The General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their
property records with the result of physical count to ensure that PPE
recorded in the property records really exist and that all existing PPE are
recorded in the books, in order to establish completeness of balances of
PPE accounts. Any variance shall be dealt accordingly and subsequently
adjusted as necessary.

2.14 We recommended that the -
• Municipal Engineer submit the Local Road Inventory and Road Map for CY
2024;
• Municipal General Services Officer maintain a Local Road Inventory and
Road Map and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all
roads and its components; and
• The Municipal Accountant:
➢ Keep and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Card for roads and road
components for every road network and reconcile the same with the
result of the physical inventory taking at the end of the year; and
➢ See to it that the required information on Road Network System be
disclosed in the Notes to FS.

3.16 We recommended that the:
• Municipal Treasurer:
❖ Adjust the noted reconciling items in the cashbooks to bring correct
balances; and
❖ Take extra care in recording transactions, footing of balances and
ensure completeness of recorded transactions to come up with
accurate balances.
• Municipal Accountant:
❖ Analyze and trace back the above noted differences per FS, draw the
necessary adjusting journal entries to bring the correct balance of Cash
in Bank-LCCA account; and
❖ Prepare monthly reconciliation of cashbooks, bank and ledger
balances for all MLGU’s bank accounts, pursuant to Section 74 of PD
No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011. Forward a copy to the Municipal
Treasurer for her information and perusal. Also, submit a copy of the
bank reconciliation statement to the Audit Team for verification, along
with the bank statements and Journal Entry Voucher for the needed
adjustments.
• Municipal Accountant and Treasurer regularly reconcile their records, or at
least quarterly, to thresh out any errors or variances to ensure accurate
cash in bank balances.

4.18 We recommended that the:
• Municipal Accountant:
❖ Analyze the above-discussed errors and draw adjusting entries to bring
the correct balances of the accounts.
❖ Maintain subsidiary ledger for each grant of cash advance to easily
monitor erroneous recording and unliquidated cash advances;
• Municipal Treasurer review and adjust the entries in the cash advance
cashbooks to reflect the correct balances of her accountability. Any
unused cash advance be forwarded as balance, and the liquidation will
only be recorded upon consummation of the purpose with supporting
documents and or refund thereof;
• Management:
❖ Ensure that payrolls be properly checked to avoid unnecessary refund
and unliquidated balances;
❖ Demand the Municipal Treasurer to immediately refund / submit
liquidations for her unliquidated balances (Item 4.13);
❖ Direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to immediately submit
the unsubmitted Liquidation Documents (Item 4.14), for audit purposes.
• Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer observe proper recording in the
cashbooks to reflect the correct balance of her accountability at any point
in time. Municipal Accountant and Treasurer reconcile their records at
least quarterly, to thresh out any errors / omissions / differences, and
immediately adjust / effect the same to ensure accurate cash advances
balances.

5.17 We recommended that the:
• Municipal Accountant:
❖ Analyze the foregoing errors and draw correcting journal entries to
bring correct account balances. For the ₱85,000.00 (Table 6.2)
recorded deposit of excessive cash advance, determine what
particular cash advance was refunded to determine proper
accounting thereof;
• Management:
❖ Instruct the Treasury Office to re-create the cash advance cashbook
for July 2019 to December 2019. Since most entries per Cash
Disbursements Journals matched with the submitted liquidation
documents, the Cash Disbursement Journals be the basis for the
recording in the cashbook; For those recorded in the Cash
Disbursement Journals not bearing the name of the payee, these be
evaluated before recording the same in the cashbook;
❖ Cause the immediate submission of the Liquidation Documents
aggregating ₱3,938,943.44 (Item 5.13, net of no payees), to the Audit
Team for examination; and
❖ Establish correctly the accountability of the former Acting MT and
 demand him to liquidate and or refund the balance of his
accountability.

6.8 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant:
• Look into the above noted lapses and prepare/draw necessary adjusting
entry in the CBs and books of accounts to capture complete transactions
and to bring the correct balances of Cash Local Treasury account;
• Determine the correct balance of undeposited collections and the
responsible accountable officer, and demand immediate deposit thereof;
• Reconcile regularly the treasury and accounting records to thresh out
discrepancies and to avoid accumulation of any differences and to
establish accurate balances; and
• For the Municipal Treasurer, observe proper recording of collections and
deposits in the CBs, foot and close the cashbooks at the end of each month
to readily determine accountability balances.

7.8 We recommended that the:
• Municipal Accountant submit the SSMI and Acknowledgment Receipts as
well as the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories to support the above
recorded expenses and inventory balances. In the event that the
inventories were already distributed, proper documentation be made and
subsequent adjusting entry to adjust the inventory and recognize the
expenses be drawn in the books; and
• Supply Officer / Municipal Treasurer maintain inventory records and
prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued and submit the
same to the Municipal Accountant for her bases in recording the expenses
in the books. Likewise, the Department Heads in-charged for such LGU
programs secure Acknowledgment Receipt from the beneficiaries and
forward the same to the Municipal Accountant to support the entry for the
distribution of the goods; and
• Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant provide proper documentation for
the issuance of supplies and goods for distributions. The SSMI or
Acknowledgment Receipt be attached to the JEV to support the validity of
recorded expenses; and
• Further, Management see to it the Report of the Physical Count of
Inventories to support inventory balances, if any, be submitted to the Audit
Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and
second semesters, respectively, for verification purposes.

8.7 We recommended that Management –
• Submit the covering MOA along with its Work and Financial Plan and SB
Resolution authorizing the LCE to enter into MOA for the above-tabulated
fund transfers, for examination purposes;
• Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Subsidiary
Ledger for each fund received per source agency for effective monitoring
of fund utilization and balances; and
• In the future, see to it that the MOA /Trust Agreement along with its Work
and Financial Plan / approved Program of Work (for infra) and SB
Resolution authorizing the LCE to enter into MOA be submitted to the
Audit Team, or attached as supporting documents to the contract and
disbursement voucher charged from the fund.

9.12 We recommended that the:
• Municipal Accountant draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify the
semi-expendable items purchased in 2023 and prior years and the related
Accumulated Depreciation to the appropriate accounts;
• General Service Officer issue the corresponding ICS to establish
accountability over these tangible items which were already issued to the
end-users;
• Management direct the Municipal Accountant and General Service Officer
to comply with the provisions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2022-004
dated May 31, 2022 and COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024.

10.10 We recommended that Management -
• Instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort to ascertain and collect
the accounts Due from NGAs and Due from GOCCs (Due from LGUs).
Measures include representation and confirmation with DBM and or GSIS
on the existence / veracity of the said ₱51,057.35 December 2001 IRA
which was not received and ₱999,421.48 GSIS premiums deducted from
IRA. Only when it is definitely determined said accounts to be
uncollectible that a request for authority to write-off the accounts together
with all the necessary documents be submitted before the Commission;
and
• In the future, for transactions involving receivables, instruct the Municipal
Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers in accordance with Section
4(e) of the NGAS to provide complete information on the accounts and to
attain fair presentation of financial statements.

11.10 We recommended that the:
• Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer look into their records and
always ensure that Registries, SAAOB and SCBAA are properly
presented;
• Municipal Accountant include the required Reconciliation in the Notes
to Financial Statements, to come up with more reliable and informative
Financial Statements.

12.15 We recommended that:
• Management:
❖ Determine the cause of delay and address the same in order to
comply with the required submission dates of accounts and reports;
❖ Cause the immediate submission of unsubmitted accounts, under
Tables 12.1 and 12.2;
• The Municipal Treasurer submit monthly accounts - the Report of Checks
Issued (check DVs), Report of Disbursements (Cash DVs, Payrolls) and
Report of Collections and Deposits together with their supporting
vouchers, payrolls and other documents to the Accounting Office within
the prescribed period set forth under the Updated LTOM. Transmittal
Letter be prepared and a received copy thereof be furnished to the audit
team for reference; and
• The Municipal Accountant see to it that accounts and financial
statements be submitted to the audit team within the prescribed periods
pursuant to Section 347 of Republic Act No. 7160, Paragraph 7 of COA
Circular No. 2009-006, Section 70 of NGAS for LGUs and Item 4 of COA
Circular No. 2010-01 for timely examination of accounts and
communication/ correction of possible errors. Monthly accounts be
submitted along with the journals and financial statements and trial
balances to facilitate systematic audit and verification.

13.5 We recommended that Management submit the covering SB Resolution and
duly notarized Deeds of Donation, and the JEV dropping the said assets from
the MLGU’s books of accounts, stamped received by the end-users.
13.6 We reiterated our recommendation that:
• For the School Building, coordinate with the Abra DepEd officials for the turn-
over, and subsequently furnish the DepEd Accountant a copy of the JEV
dropping said School Building along with the supporting documents; and
• For the Barangay Hall, instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the
recording thereof in the books of the Barangays.

We recommended that Management:
 For the 144 DVs and LDs totaling
₱24,034,006.62 (Table 2), submit the
required supporting documents to
establish the validity of the transactions
and payments;
 Submit immediately the 136 DVs and LDs
amounting to ₱22,119,965.63, ₱129,926.08 receipts and 54 cancelled
summarized under Table 3 for verification; to
 Determine the cause of delay and address
the same in order to comply with the
required submission dates of accounts and
reports; and
 Henceforth, ensure submission of accounts
and financial statements together with
journals within the prescribed periods to
facilitate timely audit and examination as
well as communication of possible
errors/deficiencies for Management’s
corrective actions.

We recommended that the:
 Municipal Engineer and the Municipal
General Services Officer conduct a yearly
physical count of local road network
projects and accordingly update the local
road map of the MLGU;
 Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal General
Services Officer the description and cost
segregation of road components of the
road infrastructure projects for their bases
in recording in the Local Road Network
Ledger Card and maintenance of Local
Road Network Property Card,
respectively;
 Municipal General Services Officer maintain
a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and
keep a complete Local Road Network
Property Card for all roads and its
components; and
 The Municipal Accountant keep and
maintain Local Road Network Ledger Card
for roads and road components for every
road network and reconcile the same with
the result of the physical inventory taking at
the end of the year. Also, provide for the
depreciation for the local road network and
disclosure in the Notes to the Financial
Statements.

We recommended that the:
 Management -
 Create an Inventory Committee to conduct
complete annual physical count of PPE in
accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS
Manual for LGUs, Vol. 1. A
representative of the Auditor be invited to
witness the inventory taking for this
purpose;
 See to it that the Inventory Committee
prepare the Report on the Physical Count
of Property, Plant and Equipment
(RPCPPE) which shall be reconciled with
the PPE records kept by the of the General
Services Office and Accounting Office;
and that a copy of the RPCPPE shall be
submitted to COA for verification not later
than January 31 of each year pursuant to
Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS;
 The General Services Officer maintain PPE
Cards and ARE/PAR and ensure that the
ARE/PAR be renewed every after three
years pursuant to Section 492 of GAAM,
Volume 1, to establish effective management
and control over PPE;
 The Municipal Accountant maintain complete
and updated PPELC for each category of
PPE pursuant to Section 114 of NGAS
Manual for LGUs to provide ready
information on the accounts and to support
balances in the financial statements;
 The General Services Officer and Municipal
Accountant reconcile their property records
with the result of physical count to ensure
that PPE recorded in the property records
really exists and that all existing PPE are
recorded in the books, in order to establish
correctness and completeness of balances
of PPE accounts. Any variance shall be dealt
accordingly and subsequently adjusted as
necessary.

We recommended that the:
 Municipal Accountant:
 Analyze the foregoing errors and draw
correcting journal entries to bring correct
account balances;
 Ensure proper recording of transactions in
the journals and accurate posting and
summarizing in the ledgers to come up
with accurate account balances;
 Acting Municipal Treasurer:
 Correct the entries in the cash advance
Cashbook (CB);
 Ensure proper recording of transactions in
the cashbook; the DV Number, payee and
amount be correctly recorded to arrive at
accurate balance of accountability;
 Maintain separate cashbook for payroll,
special purpose and operating expenses to
easily establish accountability and facilitate
reconciliation of records with the books of
accounts;
 MA and Acting MT reconcile their records at
least quarterly to thresh out discrepancies
and subsequently adjust their records as
necessary, to fairly present balances.

We recommended that the:
 Municipal Accountant:
 Ensure proper recording of transactions in
the journals and accurate posting and
summarizing in the ledgers to come up
with accurate account balances;
 reconcile the cash in bank ledger balance
with bank and cashbook balances to
establish correct balance of the account.
 Acting Municipal Treasurer foot correctly
monthly balances of his cashbooks and see
to it the same previous month’s balance be
forwarded on the ensuing months to
reflect accurate balance thereof;
 MA and Acting MT reconcile their records at
least quarterly to thresh out discrepancies
and adjust their records as necessary, to
fairly present balances.

We recommended that Management:
 Instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert
effort to ascertain and collect the accounts
Due from NGAs and Due from GOCCs. Measures include representation and
confirmation with DBM and or GSIS on
the existence / veracity of the said
₱51,057.35 December 2001 IRA which was
not received and ₱999,421.48 GSIS
premiums deducted from IRA. Only when
it is definitely determined said accounts to
be uncollectible that a request for
authority to write-off the accounts together
with all the necessary documents be
submitted before the Commission; and
 In the future, for transactions involving
receivables, instruct the Municipal
Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers
in accordance with Section 4(e) of the
NGAS to provide complete information on
the accounts and to attain fair presentation
of financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal
Accountant-
 Analyze the undocumented payables,
determine those which are valid
obligations of the MLGU, and
subsequently draw adjusting entry, as
necessary; and
 In the future, for transactions involving
payables, maintain subsidiary ledgers in
accordance with Section 4(e) of the NGAS
to provide complete information on the
accounts and to attain fair presentation of
financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal
Accountant:
 Submit the DV under check no. 968119
dated June 13, 2019, analyze the above
transactions and journal entries and
subsequently prepare necessary adjusting
journal entries to come up with the correct
balances of Loans Payable-Domestic; and
 Always ensure that all journal entries
relating to loan amortizations be duly
documented to support its propriety and
validity.

We recommended that the Municipal
Accountant classify the current and non-current
portions of the Loans Payable-Domestic and
accordingly present it in the Statement of
Financial Position. This is to help management
in the decision-making process as well as in the
financial planning, particularly in setting up
priorities for funding in payments of currently
maturing obligations.

We recommended that the Municipal
Accountant:
 See to it that the amount set-up for RPT
Receivables (also for SET Receivables) at
the beginning of the year be supported
with list of tax payers pursuant to Section
20 of NGAS to establish the correctness of
collectibles;
 Analyze the balances of RPT Receivables
and Deferred Real Property Tax Income
whether collections during the year are
included in the beginning balances, and
effect necessary adjustments to bring the
correct balances of the accounts;
 Coordinate with the Acting Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to
establish correctness of collectible real
property taxes and determine any
changes/updates in the real properties.

We recommended that the Municipal
Budget Officer comply with the requirements
of properly accomplishing the Obligation
Requests for all expenditures. Ensures that the
Registry of Appropriations, Allotment and
Obligations be maintained to properly monitor
the budget of the agency and serve as the basis
to prepare the Statement of Allotment,
Appropriations, Obligations and Balances;
The Municipal Accountant:
 Do not process claims without the required
certification as to the availability of
appropriation including the specific cost
centers or function/program/project where
expenses will be charged; and
 See to it that the SCBAA be supported with
Reconciliation with the Statement of
Financial Performance to adequately inform
users on the utilization of funds and
incurrence of expenditures.

We recommended that Management exert
effort to demand from the concerned municipal
officials and employees, including those who
are no longer connected with MLGU-Lagayan,
the refund of the withholding of taxes and loan
amortizations paid on their behalf. Remedies
include deduction from their monthly salary and
terminal leave benefit payments.

We recommended that Management instruct
the Municipal Treasurer to pay disbursements
exceeding ₱15,000.00 and all infrastructure or
other undertakings on a project basis through
checks directly to the payee in compliance with
Sections 2.2 and 4.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-
002 and Section 174(g) of GAAM Volume1.

We recommended that Management adhere to
the transparency provisions on the information
and publicity of programs and projects set forth
under COA Circular COA Circular No. 2013-004.
The quarterly report on publicized projects be
submitted to the Audit Team for audit purposes.

We recommended that management submit to
COA the NADAI along with the list of
programs and projects and approved Notices of
Supplemental Budget covering the Share from
Tobacco Excise Tax for verification.

We recommended that the: 
 Sanggunian Bayan (SB) pass a resolution
authorizing the Municipality of Lagayan to
open a separate depository bank account for
the Share from Tobacco Excise Tax.
 Municipal Accountant maintain separate
books of accounts for the said fund as well as
establish a complete set of subsidiary ledgers
to account for its transactions for more
effective monitoring of utilization.

We recommended that Management, henceforth,
direct the Municipal Accountant to attach
checklist of documentary requirements for
every transaction/DV and ensure that the
required documents be submitted,
accomplished and properly reviewed before
attending payments. In case of lacking
documents, the DV be returned to the
requesting department for completion.

We recommended that Management and
Municipal Accountant see to it that succeeding
transaction be properly supported with
complete documentation in order to establish
the validity of claims. Attending payment of
claims that are not properly documented be
discontinued.

We recommended that Management:
 Submit proof of payment and supporting
documents as required by the nature of the
transaction pursuant to COA Circular No.
2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, to support the
validity and propriety of the disbursements;
 For the personal expenses amounting to
₱1,105.00, identify the claimant and demand
for the refund, which should be receipted and
deposited to the account of the MLGU; and
 Always see to it that all claims be covered
by a Disbursement Voucher along with its
supporting papers to establish validity of
claims and ensure that not a single centavo
from municipal coffers be unconscionably
paid for personal expenses.

We recommended that Management always
ensure that Job Orders/Casuals are backed up
by the required SB Resolution and covering
contacts and that the daily wage payrolls are
supported with duly approved DTRs and
validated accomplishment reports before
payment is effected to establish the validity of
the transactions.

We recommended that Management:
 Submit the lacking or needed information/
documents discussed in Items 22.5 to 22.18
herein to the audit team for verification;
 Always see to it that all disbursement
vouchers relating to payments of infra
projects be properly documented and that
such were duly accomplished in consonance
with pertinent laws, rules and regulations to
establish the propriety and validity of the
transactions. Processing and payment of
claims that are not sufficiently documented
be discontinued.

We recommended that the BAC Chairperson:
 Submit for verification the contracts and
its integral parts for the infra projects
totaling ₱30,210,261.601.60, as well as the
lacking documentary evidences
enumerated under Item 23.6 for contracts
amounting to ₱22,255,184.91, to support the
validity of the award of the contracts and
eventually the payments thereof; and
 Always ensure that all contracts be
supported with complete documentations
required under RA No. 9184 and Its IRR
and other relevant guidelines on
procurement.

We recommended that the BAC see to it
Invitation to Bid and all related procurement
documents and notices be posted in the
PhilGEPS pursuant to Section 8.1.1 of the IRR
to attain widest dissemination of opportunities
and uphold transparency in government
procurement.

We recommended that the BAC Chairperson
see to it that all Bidding Documents, Notice of
Award and Notice to Proceed, and other related
procurement-notices be posted in the PhilGEPS
portal. Ensure to fully use the system in the
accordance with pertinent provisions of RA
No. 9184 and its IRR and GPPB issuances.

We recommended that Management always
requires future winning bidders to submit
performance security with validity period that
include the one-year defects liability period
pursuant to Section 39 of the 2016 IRR of RA
9184 and GPPB Resolution No. 012-2005 to
protect the interests of the MLGU. The validity
period shall commence from the effectivity of
the contract up to one year after the completion
of the project or up to the issuance of the
Certificate of Acceptance.

We recommended that Management:
 Submit the approved Municipal School
Board Budget for CY 2019, if any, and/or
justification why such expenses were paid
without the corresponding budget; and
 Henceforth, follow strictly the provisions of
Section 99 of R.A. No. 7160 and Article 184
of the IRR of the Local Government Code
before the disbursement of the said fund.

We recommended that Management revisit and
adhere with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC
No. 2017-1 on the appropriation and utilization
of the 20% of the IRA for programming of
development projects. Consider utilizing the
fund for projects that yield lasting benefit to the
constituents of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management appoint a
Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management
Officer to spearhead the effective and
efficient implementation and monitoring of
disaster related activities; as well as, to
oversee preparation and submission of required
reports to COA and to other oversight agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal
Accountant and MDRRM Officer-designate
submit the monthly Report on the Receipt and
Utilization of the DRRMF and for CY 2019 to
COA on or before the 15th day after the end of
each month pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA
Circular No. 2012-002 for verification. Also, it
should be supported with schedule detailing the
disbursements covered to facilitate evaluation.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person:
 Submit to the audit team the GAD AR for
CY 2019 along with the following
accompanying reports pursuant to Item V of
COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18,
2014 and Section 4.5.C.8 of PCW-DILG-
DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended
by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-
01: (a) brief summary of the reported
program or project; (b) copies of reported
policy issuances; (c) results of HGDG tests;
and (d) actions taken by the LGU on the COA
audit findings and recommendations.
 Always see to it GAD AR and accompanying
reports be submitted to the assigned Audit
Team within five (5) working days from the
end of the January of the preceding year
pursuant to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-
001 for verification.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point
System -
 Institutionalize the GAD database with
adequate and systematically gathered sex-
disaggregated data which will provide the
bases for gender analysis, planning,
programming and policy formulation; and
 Undertake gender analysis to identify and
confirm existing gender issues and ensure
that the programs, projects and activities are
responsive to the said issues.

We recommended that Management establish
and/or strengthen their monitoring and
evaluation systems to assess the effectiveness
and efficiency of their gender mainstreaming
efforts, and the M&E activities shall be
included in the annual GPB.

We recommended that the Management require
the respective employees to refund the PAG-
IBIG contributions remitted on their behalf, see
Annex P for the details.

We recommended that Management submit
documentary evidences to support the validity
of the refunds made to the above municipal
employees. Henceforth, ensure that validity of
any refund be fully established before attending
payments thereof.

We recommended that Management instruct
the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to
deduct, withhold and remit the corresponding
VAT/NVAT/EWT on all its purchases of goods,
infrastructure and services as mandated by
laws, rules and regulations to avoid impositions
of sanctions by the Bureau of Internal Revenue.

We recommended that Management:
 Instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to
keep subsidiary ledgers, index cards or work
paper to easily tract and check whether the
amount deducted was totally remitted or the
amount remitted was already more than the
amount deducted;
 Direct the Municipal Accountant and Acting
Municipal Treasurer to determine and
compute correctly deductions from salaries of
employees and ensure that these shall be
remitted accordingly to BIR, GSIS, HDMF and
PHIC;
 Update record especially salary increases of
municipal officials and employees with these
institutions, as necessary, for them to avail
and enjoy the full benefits thereto;
 Always remit the taxes, GSIS, HDMF and
PHIC premiums and amortizations, in
accordance with the guidelines set forth
therein, to avoid imposition of surcharges and
penalties and employees’ inconveniences in
availing of the benefits and services of the
concerned institutions; and
 For any interests/penalty/ surcharges that
may be imposed by the concerned
agency/institution, this should not be charged
from the coffers of the MLGU; instead, should
be shouldered by the responsible personal
pursuant to above pertinent provisions.

We recommended that the Municipal
Accountant-designate analyze and trace back
the possible nature and composition of the
beginning balance of Due to BIR account. After
which, prepare the necessary adjustments to
bring the correct balance of the account.

We have recommended that henceforth, the
Municipal Accountant-designate see to it that
the GJs be accompanied by JEVs to support the
validity and the correctness of accounting
entries made.

We recommended that Management submit -
 Copies of the covering perfected contracts
and supporting documents to COA for audit
purposes. Henceforth, ensure the submission
of perfected contracts within the prescribed
five (5) working days from execution thereof
pursuant to Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular No.
2009-001, for a systematic and effective
review process to come up with timely and
relevant audit results.
 Lacking documents cited under Item 13.7
herein to COA for audit purposes. Henceforth,
see to it that all disbursement vouchers be
properly accompanied by the required
documents and that such were duly
accomplished in accordance with Item 4.6 of
PD No. 1445, to establish the validity of the
transactions. Processing and payment of
claims that are not sufficiently documented be
discontinued.

30.4 We have recommended that Management enforce settlements of the
above suspensions and disallowances.
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant–Designate compute and provide depreciation based on the estimated useful life of assets as provided under COA Circular 2003-007. Building.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to locate and submit the missing documents. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should see to it that monthly reports and journals be submitted to the Auditor for the conduct of timely audit so as to facilitate timely communication of noted deficiencies. This way, management could easily pinpoint the persons responsible and determine the causes of delay of submission of accounts and reports.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare transmittal of submitted vouchers to the Municipal Account, who after recording in the Books of Accounts, will then submit the same to the Auditor for the conduct of timely audit so as to facilitate timely communication of noted deficiencies. For CY 2018, submission was still delayed, discussed in Part II, Observation No. 9. For CY 2019, accounts for January to July were already submitted. But the FS and journals are not yet submitted.
We have recommended that limit the grant of Cash Advance to the Municipal Treasurer for payroll and petty operating expenses only and withdrawals should be supported with list of estimated expenses.
We have recommended that all disbursements exceeding ₱15,000.00 shall be made by check and paid directly to the payee. Establish a petty cash fund for operating expenses of small amount.

We have recommended that management prepare its APP and supporting PPMPs to serve as a guide in the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects and furnish COA of the same for audit/evaluation purposes. No procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with the approved APP of the procuring entity.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections regularly and to deposit the balance of ₱120,023.59 as of December 31, 2017 and henceforth abide by the rules and regulations on deposit of collections pursuant to Section 32 of the Manual on NGAS, Section 111 of GAAM Vol. 1 and DBM-DOF Joint Cir. 1-81.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer submit duly accomplished supporting documents as required by the afore-cited regulations. Succeeding transactions should be properly supported with complete documentation in order to establish the validity of claims. Processing and payment of claims that are not properly documented should be discontinued.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the SB authorization/resolution, and contract of services/job orders as required under the aforementioned regulations. We have also recommended that the Municipal Accountant evaluate and see to it that payrolls were supported with complete documents before payment is effected to establish the validity and legality of the transactions.
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor hire qualified personnel for the mandatory positions as provided under R.A. No. 7160 in order to improve the conduct of operations of the LGU.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require the submission of daily time records, leave forms and other pertinent documents to support the payment of salaries to employees and refrain from paying salaries to persons other than the employees without proper authorization. Payments of salary without the signature of the concerned employee or without proper authorization and payments of net take home pay below Four Thousand Pesos should be stopped.

We have recommended the following: Ensure that expenditures are limited only to what is appropriated; Ensure that the required registries are maintained by the Municipal Accountant or the Budget Officer to prevent the incurrence of expenditures in excess of allotments; Prepare the Municipal Budget in such a way that actual needs of the municipality are properly included and allocated with funds in the budget; and The Obligation Requests which is the basis for recording of obligations in the registries should be properly accomplished and the four RAAOs should be maintained and updated to facilitate monitoring of expenses and balances of appropriations.
We have recommended that the management justify the noted lapses and henceforth, adhere to the transparency provisions as provided by the different rules and regulations cited above.

We have recommended that management explain the noted lapses and submit the lacking documentations. Henceforth, avoid splitting of contracts and payments. Purchases beyond the threshold on shopping and small value procurements should be done through competitive bidding as mandated under R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR. No splitting and beyond threshold were noticed.

We have recommended that management establish their Local Council for the Protection of Children who shall prepare plans and ensure implementation of the same in compliance with the provisions of Section 15 of RA 9344 and DILG MC 2012-120.
We have recommended that management form a Solid Waste Management Board that will prepare Solid Waste Management Plan that will be in compliance with Republic Act 9003.

We have recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council headed by the Municipal Mayor allocate thirty percent for the Quick Response Fund out of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund for relief and recovery programs during disaster and calamities. The 30%-70% allocation should be properly identified in the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Investment Plan and in the disbursements for monitoring of implementation. We also recommended the submission of the list of beneficiaries of the goods distributed and only activities in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment should be followed/implemented.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer comply with the regulations on the transfer of unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF to the Trust Fund. We have also recommended that the Sanggunian Bayan pass a resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer to open a separate Special Trust Fund for the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF. Further, the Municipal Accountant should maintain a special account and a complete set of subsidiary ledger for the said funds. The unused capital outlay of the fund should remain in the General Fund books.
We have recommended that management should appropriate and utilize an amount for procuring rescue and response equipment that can be used during calamity to mitigate the adverse effect of disasters.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint personnel to handle the responsibilities of a Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (LDRRMO) who shall prepare and submit a monthly utilization report on DRRMF. Management should familiarize themselves on rules and regulations pertaining to disaster risk reduction management.

We have recommended that management submit monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of the DRRMF sourced from GAA and Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations of the municipality to NDRRMC through the OCD on or before the 5th day following the end of each month, and furnish copy to the audit team for audit/evaluation.

We have recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator monitor and ensure that the projects under the Annual Investment Plan are implemented as planned and update the plan in cases where projects included in the plan are not in accordance with the purpose of the 20% Development Plan.

We have recommended that the utilization of the 20% Development Fund be strictly limited to programs, projects and activities that will contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures and yield greater benefit to the people in accordance with DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

We have recommended that the municipality submit GPBs to the PPO of the Provincial Government to ensure the alignment of the municipal GAD PPAs with the priorities of the province, and that the GAD Accomplishment Report and its accompanying documents shall be furnished to COA for audit as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014. We also recommend that the LGU establish its GFPS to ensure that GAD mechanisms and processes are in place. Also, create GAD M&E Team who shall evaluate the outcomes of the LGU GAD policies, programs and projects in accordance with Item 5.1 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended. Moreover, establish and/or strengthen the monitoring and evaluation systems to assess the effectiveness and efficiency and the M&E activities shall be included in the annual GPB.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant deduct, withhold and remit the corresponding VAT/NVAT/EWT on all its future purchases and payments for contracts as mandated by laws, rules and regulations to avoid impositions of sanctions by the Bureau of Internal Revenue. We further recommend that purchases through cash or goods reimbursements should be minimized if not totally eliminated.

We have recommended the following: The Municipal Accountant should exert more effort to establish existence and determine nature of the accounts. Measures which including sending demand letters to debtors reminding them of their obligations should be made. If the receivables would be found uncollectible and unrecoverable, it should be written-off subject to rules and regulations of the COA. The request for authority to write-off the accounts outstanding for more than ten years should be forwarded to the COA for evaluation as required by existing rules and regulations. Also, the accounting unit should maintain complete and updated subsidiary ledgers for Receivables in accordance with Section 4(e) of the NGAS.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer explain the frequent cancellations of checks and refrain from or avoid cancelling checks when they are not actually torn, mutilated, defaced or with erasures/errors pursuant to rules and regulations of the NGAS. Likewise, checks must be issued/recorded in chronological order pursuant to laws, rules and regulations.

We have recommended that to avoid the incurrence of irregular and unnecessary expenses, the Municipality should not maintain the Lagayan Extension Office to save payment of utility expenses for offices not used. We also recommended that the management compel the persons responsible for the payments to refund the unnecessary and irregular expenditures.

We have recommended that management explain the noted lapses on procurement of goods. Hence, all procurements of the municipality above ₱100,000.00 shall be done through public bidding as mandated under R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR, unless justified by the conditions provided under Section 52 of the IRR and as recommended by the BAC and duly approved by the head of the procuring entity. Alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted to only in highly exceptional cases provided for in R.A. No. 9184. We have recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant should exert more effort to establish existence and determine nature of the accounts.  Measures which include sending demand letters to debtors reminding them of their overdue obligations should be made.  If the receivables would be found uncollectible and unrecoverable, it should be written-off subject to rules and regulations of the COA.  The request for authority to write-off the accounts outstanding for more than 10 years should be forwarded to the COA for evaluation as required by existing rules and regulations; and
Also, the accounting unit should maintain complete and updated subsidiary ledgers for Receivables in accordance with Section 4(e) of the NGAS.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant-designate and Municipal Treasurer to submit the vouchers in accordance with the provisions of R.A. No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 to avoid penalty under the Revised Penal Code and possible disallowance of the transaction.  Henceforth, monthly accounts should be submitted on time.

We have recommended that payments above ₱15,000.00 be made through the issuance of checks and paid directly to the payee in accordance with the above pertinent rules and regulations. This would not only lessen the cash advances of the Municipal Treasurer but it would facilitate verification of legality and correctness of her accountability.

We have recommended that copies of the required documents be submitted to the Auditor pursuant to COA circular 2009-001 and 96-006 to facilitate proper evaluation of procurement transactions and also to prevent audit suspensions and or disallowances.
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor hire qualified personnel for the mandatory positions as provided under R.A. No. 7160 in order to improve the conduct of operations of the LGU.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare transmittal of submitted vouchers to the Municipal Account, who after recording in the Books of Accounts, will then submit the same to the Auditor for the conduct of timely audit so as to facilitate timely communication of noted deficiencies. This way, management could easily pinpoint the persons responsible and determine the causes of delay of submission of accounts and reports.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer explain the frequent cancellations of checks  and refrain from or avoid cancelling checks when they are not actually torn, mutilated, defaced or with erasures/errors pursuant to rules and regulations of the NGAS. Likewise, checks must be issued/recorded in chronological order pursuant to laws, rules and regulations.

We have recommended the following:

That management justify the above noted lapses.  The Municipal Accountant - Designate and Municipal Treasurer submit duly accomplished supporting documents as required by regulations. Processing and payment of claims that are not properly documented should be discontinued.  Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer should effect payments only on vouchers that are properly documented; and  

On repair and maintenance, the management should be guided with the rules and regulations particularly on the purchase of spare parts and repair of transportation equipment.  Records of repair for each transportation equipment should be maintained to avoid unnecessary and excessive expenses for repairs.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the department heads to prepare their Annual Procurement Plan to be consolidated by Agency through its BAC-TWG. Moreover, the agency should take into consideration the existence of appropriation before incurring expenditures in conformity with rules and regulations.

We have recommended that management explain the noted lapses on procurement of goods. No. 9184 and its IRR, unless justified by the conditions provided under Section 52 of the IRR and as recommended by the BAC and duly approved by the head of the procuring entity.  Alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted to only in highly exceptional cases provided for in R.A. No. 9184.  In all instances, the procuring entity shall ensure that the most advantageous price for the LGU is obtained.

LGUs shall establish and/or strengthen their monitoring and evaluation systems to assess the effectiveness and efficiency of their gender mainstreaming efforts, and the M&E activities shall be included in the annual GPB.

The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and Property Custodian the description and cost segregation of road components of various road infrastructure projects; and
